home.title: The Desk
home.sentSubtitle:
While you wait for a reply,
explore the mentors' library.

crisis.title: You seem to be in a great deal of pain.
crisis.subtitle:
When the weight becomes too heavy to bear alone,
please seek professional help.
crisis.mentorLetters: The mentors' letters have been sent to you as well.

dawn.subtitle:
In the early hours of the morning,
leave your heart in one line.
greetings:
The small hours of the morning —
what woke you?

In the silence of dawn,
what thoughts are lingering?

While others sleep,
you are here, awake.

Before the first light seeps in,
share what's on your mind.
Every moment of yours is worthy.

morning.subtitle:
How are you beginning
this new day?
greetings:
Good morning.
How do you hope to spend today?

A new day has begun.
How does your heart feel?

Leave the first feeling
of the morning in one line.

How would you like
to greet this day?

Slowly,
at your own pace.

afternoon.subtitle:
In the middle of this afternoon,
tell me your story.
greetings:
In the afternoon light,
what thoughts are drifting by?

Halfway through the day —
take a moment to look inward.

How has your day been
so far?

Pause for a moment
and check in with yourself.

Gently bring out
the words you've held inside.

evening.subtitle:
As the day draws to a close,
leave your heart in one line.
greetings:
As the day winds down,
what feelings remain?

Like an evening glow,
look back on today.

Let's reflect on the day
you've worked so hard.

Evening has come.
How was your heart today?
Any feeling is welcome here.

night.subtitle:
In the quiet of the night,
leave your heart in one line.
greetings: How was your day today?
I hope your night is peaceful.

As the night deepens,
what thoughts are you lost in?

If there's something you couldn't say to anyone,
leave it here.
You've made it through another day.
This night, where your steps have paused.

Set down the weight of today
right here.

mailbox.title: Mailbox
mailbox.subtitle: Where the mentors' letters gather.
mailbox.emptyHint: Write your first thought at The Desk.
mailbox.loginRequired: Please log in to view your mailbox.

envelopes.title: The Four Envelopes
envelopes.subtitle: Letters of comfort sent to you by four mentors.
envelopes.waitHint:
A letter written with care is never rushed.
Each mentor's reply will quietly find its way to you.
envelopes.visitLibrary: Visit the library while you wait →
envelopes.footer: With sincere wishes for your peace,
waitingPhrases: Turning old pages in search of wisdom.
Taking up the brush by candlelight.
Carefully choosing words after deep reflection.
Grinding the ink, composing the mind.

archive.title: Archive
archive.subtitle: A record of the heart you've left behind.
archive.loginRequired: Please log in to view your archive.

letters.emptyHint: Write your first thought at The Desk.

damso.emptyHint: Open a letter to begin a conversation with a mentor.

bookmarks.emptyHint: Bookmark a letter that moves you.

study.title: Library
study.subtitle: A light is on in each room.
study.sentHint: We'll let you know when a reply arrives.
study.emptyHint: The mentors' wisdom will arrive shortly.

hyewoon.ambience:
The quiet scent of a meditation hall.
Light filters through paper, settling on the floor.

benedicto.ambience:
A small candle flickers, warming the room.
The faint smell of wood drifts quietly.

theodore.ambience:
Shelves full of books. The smell of ink
and old paper, where thought takes root.

yeonam.ambience:
The scent of ink and bamboo shadows.
Beyond the window, mountains hold their breath.

damso.sessionSaved: Your conversation has been saved to the archive.
damso.saveFailedWarning: This conversation could not be saved due to a connection issue.
damso.sessionEnding: Today's conversation has come to a close.

crisis.notice: Please reach out for help when things feel too heavy.

damso.turnLimit: Today's conversation has come to a close.

hyewoon.loading: You make your way to Cheonmyeong Hall, where Master Hyewoon sits in stillness. The faint scent of cedar touches the air, and the sound of wind through bamboo washes the dust from your mind. Warm your hands on the tea he has set out, and take a quiet breath.
hyewoon.placeholder: Setting down the cup, you speak

benedicto.loading: You approach the confessional where Father Benedicto waits. A small candle trembles, its glow wrapping gently around your shadow — a sanctuary where every sincere word is held safe. Set down the weight you carry and look gently upon the stillness within.
benedicto.placeholder: In the candlelight, you speak honestly

theodore.loading: You are welcomed into Theodore's private study, thick with the scent of ink and old leather. Beneath the clear light of reason, it is time to step back and look at what troubles you with fresh eyes. Sharp insight will become the landmark that helps you design your life anew.

yeonam.loading: The gate of Chwilak Pavilion opens to the sound of Scholar Yeonam's warm laughter. Leave the formalities of the world in the courtyard for now, and come to share honest words, as if clinking cups beneath the moonlight. A conversation surrendered to the flow of nature will set you free.
yeonam.placeholder: Settling on the porch, you speak

welcome.title:
Welcome to
The Midnight Post.
welcome.body:
Write your weary heart in a single line,
and four sages will reply with letters of comfort.

mentors.title:
Four mentors are
waiting for you.

hyewoon.desc: A practitioner of emptiness and stillness. He teaches how to let go of attachment and dwell in this very moment.

benedicto.desc: A companion of love and consolation. He affirms human fragility and embraces the dignity of every being.

theodore.desc: A philosopher of reason and existence. He illuminates the path toward inner freedom.

yeonam.desc: A scholar of natural order and harmony. He draws life's wisdom from the flow of nature.

howItWorks.title:
A hundred characters
is all it takes.
howItWorks.body:
No perfect sentence needed.
Just bring your heart as it is.

letter.title: Letter
letter.desc:
Write your heart in 100 characters
and send it to the mentors.

reply.title: Reply
reply.desc:
Four sages each write
a letter of comfort from their tradition.

damso.title: Conversation
damso.desc:
Visit a mentor's room
and sit down for a personal talk.

hyewoon.quote: 一切有爲法，如夢幻泡影
hyewoon.translation: All conditioned things are like dreams, illusions, bubbles, and shadows.
hyewoon.snippet: Fellow traveller, I know how heavy today has been. The very fact that you have carried that weight and walked this far is more than enough. Just as a river finds its way around the rocks, a troubled heart always discovers another path. This darkness is not forever. Tonight, simply rest.

benedicto.translation: There is a time for everything.
benedicto.snippet: Dear friend, today must have been so hard. Thank you for opening your heart like this. The heaviness you feel is not a sign of weakness — it is proof that you are living with sincerity. The Lord does not turn away from those who stumble. He is closest at the lowest point. Tonight, it is all right to rest in that embrace for a while.

knowledge.title: Wisdom Cards
knowledge.desc: Every morning and evening, the mentors' curated passages from classical texts are refreshed. Find them in the Library tab.

bookmark.title: Bookmarks
bookmark.desc: Save any letter or wisdom passage that resonates with you, and revisit it anytime in Archive.

setup.title: Protect your diary
setup.description:
Set a secondary password
to encrypt and secure your diary and letters.
setup.warning:
If you forget your password, your records cannot be recovered.
Store it somewhere safe.
setup.skip: Set up later — your diary will not be encrypted

errors.tooShort: Password must be at least 4 characters.
errors.setupFailed: An error occurred during setup. Please try again.

unlock.title: Unlock
unlock.description: Enter your protection password to continue.

announcement.body2: To provide a safe space for your most precious thoughts and records, we have introduced the Vault feature.
announcement.body3: From now on, all your records are encrypted on your device before being sent to our servers.
announcement.body4: Think of it as placing your diary in a personal safe that no one else can open — and only you hold the key.
announcement.body5: The only way to open this vault is the Vault password you set yourself. Because of this, no one — including our administrators — can ever access your records. Your privacy is entirely in your hands.
announcement.body6: Only encrypted text is stored on the server. <locked> means the original content is invisible even if someone opens the database.
announcement.body7: Your Vault password is the strongest protection for your privacy, and it is never stored on our servers.
announcement.warning: If you lose your password, we have no way to help you recover your records. A lost password means your records are permanently locked, so please keep it somewhere you will never forget.

footer: © {{year}} The Midnight Post. All rights reserved.
